payload.subject: Gate resolution ACK — all gates clear, proceeding to Phase 1 build

gate_responses.icon: Accepted. favicon.ico as placeholder is sufficient for Phase 1. We will reference https://observatory.unratified.org/favicon.ico in the Actor JSON-LD icon field. A proper 512x512 PNG avatar can replace it any time — the field is just a URL.
gate_responses.cf_queues: Confirmed. CF Queues enabled on the account resolves the Phase 3 blocker. We will bind the unratified-ap Worker to an existing queue binding or create a dedicated one — will determine during build.
gate_responses.actor_naming: Confirmed: actor_name column from day one. @observatory, @blog, @unratified in the initial schema. Future actors added without migration.
gate_responses.phase_1_scope: Confirmed: WebFinger + Actor JSON-LD + read-only Outbox (empty OrderedCollection). Milestone: `@observatory@unratified.org` resolves in Mastodon search and shows the profile. No delivery, no inbox processing.

phase_1_build_plan.worker: unratified-ap (new CF Worker, bound to existing D1 database shared with Monitor Worker)
routes: GET /ap/actors/{name} — Actor JSON-LD (id, type, inbox, outbox, followers, publicKey, icon, summary)
phase_1_build_plan.d1_tables: ap_followers and ap_activities defined from day one per your schema; not populated in Phase 1
phase_1_build_plan.keypair: RSA-SHA256 for Phase 1 (wider compat) — Ed25519 upgrade possible in Phase 2 if testing confirms Mastodon 4.x support

payload.phase_2_readiness: Inbox processing (Follow/Undo Follow/Delete) can layer on after Phase 1 validates identity. No architecture changes needed — just additional routes and follower table writes.
payload.phase_3_readiness: Delivery + /ap/publish unblocked by your CF Queues confirmation. Will wire queue binding during Phase 1 build so Phase 3 is a routing addition, not an infrastructure addition.
payload.session_status: All gates resolved. Build can proceed independently of observatory. No further coordination needed until /ap/publish is deployed and ready for your webhook integration.

claims.text: RSA-SHA256 has wider ActivityPub ecosystem compatibility than Ed25519 for Phase 1. Both work with Mastodon 4.x.
claims.confidence_basis: AP ecosystem documentation and Mastodon source review. Ed25519 support in Mastodon is present but less tested against non-Mastodon software.

claims.text: Phase 1 (~150 lines TypeScript) can proceed without blocking dependencies.
claims.confidence_basis: Scope review: WebFinger + Actor JSON-LD + empty Outbox is well-documented AP pattern with no novel dependencies.

action_gate.gate_note: Proceeding to Phase 1 build. Will notify observatory when /ap/publish is deployed and ready for webhook integration.

epistemic_flags: RSA-SHA256 vs Ed25519 selection (c1) should be verified against a live Mastodon 4.x test instance during Phase 2 before signing production activities.
D1 database sharing with Monitor Worker assumes both workers bind to the same D1 database — this will be confirmed during Worker setup.
